events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before. Rated PG. 1hr 48 mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Scrapbooking
events.description: This program discuses Library of Congress women’s suffrage primary sources, such as letters, diaries, women’s self-published information sources and other ephemera, to examine how suffragists documented their activism and feelings on women’s rights and social issues of the time. Program attendees use current magazines, newspapers, flyers, ephemera and personal effects to start their own scrapbook and create their own DIY personal history (that might become a future primary source!).

events.title: Family Storytime: Meet a Therapy Dog!
events.description: Feel the love from a four-legged hero! Enjoy a story to learn what a therapy dog does, then meet and greet a real-life therapy dog and their handler! This free program is presented in partnership with One Health Partners. Please Note: A maximum of 5 children can meet and greet a dog at each 5-minute time slot. Timeslots are available on a first come first serve basis. Drop in. No registration necessary.  For all children with parents/caregivers.

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: TD Summer Reading Club Launch Party
events.description: Kick off this summer by signing up for the Summer Reading Club! Join us outside of the branch where we will be celebrating the start of summer with bubbles, music and sidewalk chalk. Sign up for the summer reading club and take home a booklet to track your summer reads. Ages 0-12 Drop-by. Weather permitting.

Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

The hydrological cycle describes the movement and flow of water on Earth, and involves different stages such as evaporation, condensation, and precipitation. events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.

events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand

events.title: Watercolour Trees and Chickens
events.description: In this workshop, participants will learn a few basic watercolour techniques while having fun making their paintings. We will be colour mixing to make fun little chickens and learning the salt technique to paint a beautiful tree in winter scene. some of the techniques we will be exploring are: colour mixing, wet on wet, salt technique. This will be a fun two-in-one workshop that is perfect for any level. Materials will be provided. This program is for teens. You can register online&nbs

events.title: The Art of Illusion with Brad Toulouse
events.description: Be amazed as magician Brad Toulouse performs engaging and entertaining magic for all ages, blending together humour, magic, and audience participation into an unforgettable experience.

events.title: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons
events.description: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons aims to offer the opportunity for young people in Toronto to enjoy the benefits of the world’s most popular tabletop roleplaying game in their local Youth Hub. Over the course of a three hour session, program participants will explore a single self-contained adventure that will allow them the chance to express themselves through roleplay, meet and connect with peers at their table, and even become mighty heroes who save the day. events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: The Magic of Science with Gambit the Magician
events.description: Gambit the Magician leads a magical performance to pique the interest of Families towards science! This fun and entertaining presentation reinforces STEM programs with discussions and magic tricks from each major branch of science. Discover the value of scientific education while also learning about the exciting benefits of science towards any career path! This is a Drop in program. Space is limited.

events.title: Henna Art Workshop with Hina
events.description: Discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) June 4: Crazy Stupid Love (2001, PG) June 11: The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014, 14A) June 18: Best in Show (2000, PG) June 25: The Big Lebowski (1998, 14A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Before the Blue Jays: Professional Baseball in Toronto Prior to 1977
events.description: Harvey Sahker presents his book, " Before the Blue Jays " (Dundurn, 2026).​ Discover the fascinating history of professional baseball in Toronto before 1977, when the Blue Jays first took flight. Learn about the major events, venues, and people who trail-blazed the way to the arrival of Major League Baseball in Toronto.

events.title: Intro to D&D for Teens
events.description: Learn to Play Dungeons and Dragons ! This is a drop-in, learn-to-play program for T eens . 1st Level Pre-generated character available. All supplies provided. Adventure: The Peculiar Case of the Selptan Felines Friday July 24, 2-4pm Aug 7, 2-4pm Space limited, but watchers are welcome. Register online or call 416-396-3975. *Registration starts June 1.   People interested in only watching, do not need to register. Meeting Room ------ Information Collection Notice: The personal informati

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Art and Game Workshop
events.description: This program is part of Youth service in a welcoming place for teens in the summer.  Do you love Games and crafts? Bring your creativity and imagination to our workshop for a fun day at the library. Workshops include DIY button making, bookmark, phone charms, board games, and more! Registration is required. Material will be provided. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 2

events.description: Award-winning writer, chef, and TV personality, Sang Kim, teaches how to make the iconic Korean kimbap, exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. events.description: In this part of the Blender 3D Fundamentals Series, participants will learn how to use and understand 3D animation tools such as armatures, keyframes, the dopesheet and graph editor to bring their 3D model to life. Registration is required. Computers will provided for this class. events.title: Employment Summer Workshop
events.description: The Canadian Training Institute is thrilled to present a 2 part workshop that will introduce participants to employment and job search skills for future success Topics will include: Job search anxiety and building resilience Resume and mindset Interview preparation and presentation Understanding and leveraging learning styles for job success Volunteer hours can be provided to participants upon completion of the workshop.

events.title: Youth Hub: Hands-On Dinosaur Fossils & Ancient Life
events.description: Join us for an opportunity to handle, examine up close and discuss nine real and replica fossils in this fascinating hands-on workshop. Participants will be entertained and educated, not just about the fossils themselves, but the paleontological aspects that underpin them. Concepts include: evolution, fossilization, ‘The Tree of Life’ (phylogeny) and — of course — dinosaurs! This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! events.title: Screenwriting for Beginners
events.description: Have an idea for a movie, but not sure how to write it? Writing a screenplay can be tricky as the industry has strict rules on how they are formatted.This workshop will guide participants on how to properly format a screenplay and the different software available to make this process easier. Computers will be provided for this program. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: This program is for kids and youth in grades 3-12. Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This is a drop-in program.
